mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp UNRATE rise, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in housing starts indicate a structural shift in consumer spending away from long-term assets, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with > 52% accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly increases corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days exclusively during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: The positive return correlation vanishes during expansion phases, or INDPRO growth fails to predict 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate monetary tightening expectations that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 4-year rolling window, or the signal predictive power drops below a Rank IC of 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a baseline model with > 55% directional accuracy, or the interaction effect reverses sign during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns with > 50% accuracy, or the regime-specific coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.
